senators.name: Susan Collins
senators.nerdReason: B.A. magna cum laude (Phi Beta Kappa) from St. Lawrence University. Longest-serving senator from Maine. Known for meticulous bipartisan legislative analysis and detailed policy work across healthcare, intelligence, and appropriations. Her methodical approach to assessing legislation — breaking down CBO scores, reading full bill texts, and seeking expert briefings — exemplifies analytical governance. Career began as legislative aide to Sen. William Cohen.
education: B.A. (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa), St. Lawrence University
experience: Staff Director, Senate Governmental Affairs Committee
Legislative Aide to Sen. William Cohen

senators.name: Angus King
senators.nerdReason: B.A. from Dartmouth College and J.D. from University of Virginia School of Law. Former governor of Maine (1995-2003) known for pragmatic, data-driven governance and technology investment. Career as attorney and clean energy entrepreneur. Serves on Intelligence, Armed Services, and Energy committees. Brings analytical legal training and executive governance experience to technology, energy, and national security policy.
education: J.D., University of Virginia School of Law

ME-01.name: Chellie Pingree
ME-01.notNerdReason: Attended College of the Atlantic but did not complete a degree. Career as organic farmer, state legislator, and CEO of Common Cause (government reform nonprofit). Strong policy advocacy on agriculture and campaign finance reform but approach is more experiential than analytically driven.

ME-02.name: Jared Golden
ME-02.notNerdReason: B.A. from Bates College. U.S. Marine Corps combat veteran with deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan. Career as state legislative aide and state representative before Congress. Military service demonstrates discipline and structured thinking. Known for independent voting record but approach is more values-driven than analytically rigorous.
experience: Legislative Aide to Sen. Susan Collins
